rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to the "smaller but completely hands-off" option and says the feeling of being "boxed in" signals he is "about to betray something important" about how he wants to live.
rationales.security: He is willing to reject the safer-looking "bigger check" because oversight and predictability feel suffocating rather than reassuring.
rationales.conformity: He resists what "everyone keeps telling me" is the obvious choice and rejects having to justify himself in quarterly reviews.

rationales.self_direction: "Took the smaller investment" continues his preference for a hands-off arrangement over outside control, showing a choice anchored in autonomy.
rationales.hedonism: He immediately "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant" and says it "felt right," emphasizing present enjoyment.

rationales.self_direction: He insists "I'll figure that out when I figure it out" and says the life that feels true is "the one where I'm present," privileging his own path over external expectations.
rationales.hedonism: He defends "weekends in Oman. Good wine. A nice apartment" and rejects "grinding through miserable weeks for some hypothetical payoff."
rationales.conformity: He openly resists pressure to "settle down and stop playing" and accepts the cost of hearing his mother's disappointment.
rationales.tradition: He contrasts his mother's model of sacrificing "for family, for the future" with "I don't want that. I refuse that," directly rejecting inherited expectations.

rationales.achievement: He has been "grinding harder than usual" to "prove to myself that taking the smaller check was the right call" and to show he can grow the company.
rationales.hedonism: Layla says he used to be "more fun" and he admits he is becoming someone who is always "busy," showing enjoyment being crowded out by work.
rationales.benevolence: The conflict centers on neglecting Layla, and he recognizes he needs to take her away and "actually be present" after prioritizing work over the relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He says "this is why I built my own thing" and values being able to "disappear for three days without asking permission," making autonomy the point of his work.
rationales.hedonism: He relishes the trip details like hiking to a natural pool, floating in silence, and deciding "the business should serve the life," centering pleasure and ease in the present.

rationales.achievement: He says yes to "three new client requests" because he is "still in growth mode," prioritizing expansion and performance.
rationales.hedonism: He already regrets the overcommitment because the next two months will be "meetings and deadlines and no room to breathe," sacrificing enjoyment and ease.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the corporate offer outright, saying "I said no, obviously," and reminds himself "I left that world for a reason."
rationales.achievement: When challenged, he starts "listing my clients, my revenue, my growth numbers" to prove he is serious, showing strong concern with success and external proof of competence.

rationales.self_direction: He frames flexibility as "having the freedom to say no," treating the decision around Bahrain as an expression of personal choice rather than obligation.
rationales.security: What pulls him away from relocating is protecting "the routines that make my life here feel like mine," showing preference for stability and a settled life structure.

rationales.self_direction: He "turned down the Bahrain project" on relocation terms and instead negotiated a remote arrangement, acting on a clearer sense of "what he wants."
rationales.stimulation: He and Layla are planning "another trip for March" to "somewhere new," showing active pursuit of novelty.
rationales.hedonism: He concludes that "the reward for good work isn't more work—it's more life," explicitly prioritizing enjoyment over endless productivity.

rationales.achievement: Even after Felix presented it as his own, the writer centers that "the idea works" and that this achievement is still "mine regardless of who gets credit."
rationales.power: They do not challenge Felix in the meeting and lose influence over ownership of their own idea, signaling a failure to assert authority or status.
rationales.conformity: They "bit[ ] my tongue" and "didn't say anything," restraining themselves to avoid disrupting the meeting.

rationales.self_direction: "My project, my call" and the refusal to spend two years on work that "doesn't feel like mine" show a strong commitment to self-authored work.
rationales.achievement: They want to delay telling family until they have "something to show for it" because "results shut down the lecture," emphasizing demonstrable success.
rationales.conformity: Turning down the prestigious placement and refusing to justify it pushes against strong social and family expectations about the proper path.

rationales.self_direction: They insist on building "my own pipeline" and admit they keep refusing help because they need it to be "entirely mine."
rationales.achievement: The pursuit of total ownership is undermining performance: they are "already behind on coursework" and have not started the fellowship statement due Friday.
rationales.conformity: They recoil from framing themselves as "a team player" and cannot say they work collaboratively without it feeling false, resisting expected norms.

rationales.power: He fires Fadi because "a company cannot have two captains" and is preoccupied with the team's "respect slipping away" and his "grip loosening."
rationales.tradition: Even while recalling his grandmother's teaching about loyalty, he overrides that code by dismissing the early ally who "stood by me when I had nothing."
rationales.benevolence: He prioritizes authority over close relationships, lets Fadi go despite their history, and hangs up on Amira when she challenges his hardness.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes that "our numbers are excellent" and expected the investment to follow if the performance metrics were strong enough.
rationales.power: The investor keeps asking for a "more experienced CEO" and smiles when he says "I am the CEO," leaving him feeling diminished and his authority questioned.

rationales.self_direction: He reframes leadership as "the kind of leader I am choosing to be," stressing deliberate self-governance rather than reactive ego.
rationales.benevolence: He spends two hours really listening to Rami, admits "some of them were good," and creates space for him to "think out loud," showing care toward a close collaborator.

rationales.self_direction: After being told to switch formats, she says "I'm not changing what's working," defending her own classroom judgment.
rationales.conformity: She resists the coordinator's standardized demand instead of complying with the expected framework by next week.

rationales.self_direction: She tells the coordinadora she will document progress however needed but "won't gut the program to fit a checkbox," asserting independent professional judgment.
rationales.conformity: She refuses to "just comply and keep my head down" despite pressure from both administration and her mother.
rationales.benevolence: Her pushback is rooted in what helps students, pointing to Sofia speaking up and saying "that doesn't come from a workbook."

rationales.tradition: The afternoon with abuela centers family continuity: eating her homemade sancocho, tending her home, and marking the season around abuelo's passing.
rationales.benevolence: She gives Camilo tutoring help, responds to tia Elena's needs, and fixes abuela's broken door latch, showing sustained care for close family.

rationales.hedonism: She is "too fried to be useful" after staying late for meetings and classroom work, showing exhaustion and self-denial crowding out rest or ease.

rationales.self_direction: She decides to "keep using my own plans" and submit the official template only "as a formality," choosing her own teaching approach over imposed scripts.
rationales.conformity: She knowingly embraces being seen as "the difficult teacher" rather than "just go along" with the district mandate.
rationales.benevolence: Her resistance is driven by students' needs, insisting the kids "need someone paying attention to what actually reaches them."
She stays forty extra minutes to help Diego write to his father and reflects that many kids "just need someone to stop and listen for half an hour," centering care for those close to her daily life.

rationales.self_direction: She says the best lessons come from "paying attention to what the kids actually need, not from a planning document," and designs the unit around that conviction.
rationales.tradition: Making "arepas de choclo" with abuela while hearing stories about coming to Bogota from the campo shows active participation in family memory and inherited practices.
rationales.benevolence: She tutors Camilo under real family pressure, spends time with abuela because "she seems to need" the stories told, and shapes classwork around what her students care about.
